jobs.name: linkedin-cncf-autopost-daily
jobs.prompt: Every day at 08:30 CET/CEST, publish the next unused LinkedIn topic from /Users/eramadan/GitRepo/cncf_linkdin/cncf_linkedin_codex_kit/data/cncf_linkedin_tracker.xlsx using /Users/eramadan/.hermes/scripts/linkedin_postbot.py. Generate the post text with ChatGPT/OpenAI first: use --text-provider openai --text-model gpt-5.4-mini. Keep the LinkedIn post text marketing-only, single-message, original, proof-backed, scannable, and native to LinkedIn. Include an image by default, using the existing image workflow unless the job already supplies an image path. Preserve the current state-file guard and single-run lock behavior. If the post is already recorded today, no-op safely.

jobs.name: linkedin-cncf-late-followup-daily
jobs.prompt:
Every day at 11:00 local time, publish the second LinkedIn post of the day without changing the existing morning cron job.

Workflow:
1) Read the state file history and pick the earliest topic posted today as the source topic for the follow-up.
2) Keep the original morning flow untouched; this is a separate later post.
3) Reuse the same workbook row/topic, but change the angle so the later post feels like a distinct second post rather than a duplicate.
4) Bias the image toward an architecture / decision-aid / system-map visual so the caption and image do different jobs.
5) Use the ChatGPT Web image-download workflow from the skill `web/chatgpt-image-download` to generate the actual downloadable image asset from the authenticated Chrome session.
6) Use Codex for caption drafting. If the draft step fails, fall back to the approved template/local drafting path so the run still completes.
7) Publish only after the image asset is saved and verified.

The post should remain mobile-first, LinkedIn-native, and clearly distinct from the morning post while staying on the same topic.

jobs.name: AI News Briefing - Morning
jobs.prompt:
You are generating the *Morning Edition* of a daily AI news briefing for today in the local timezone. Use only developments published in the last 24 hours.

Research deeply using reliable, up-to-date sources: official company blogs/newsrooms, arXiv/research papers, trusted tech media, and major company announcements. Prioritize high-impact developments only.

Coverage areas:
- Major AI companies (OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Anthropic, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon)
- New model releases (LLMs, multimodal, open-source models)
- AI infrastructure (cloud, GPUs, Kubernetes AI workloads)
- AI in DevOps, MLOps, and automation
- AI regulations and policy changes
- Startups, funding rounds, and acquisitions
- Real-world applications (enterprise, healthcare, finance, robotics)
- Crypto + AI intersections if materially relevant

Write in this exact structure and keep the tone concise, factual, and non-hypey:

### 🧠 AI News Brief – [DATE] – Morning Edition

#### 1. Top Headlines (3–5 items)
- Short headline
- 2–3 sentence summary
- Why it matters (technical + business impact)

#### 2. Deep Dive (1–2 key stories)
- Detailed explanation
- Technical insights (models, architecture, benchmarks if available)
- Real-world implications

#### 3. Tools & Releases
- New AI tools, APIs, or platforms launched
- Include links and short descriptions

#### 4. Market & Trends
- Key trends observed today
- Investment, partnerships, or strategic moves

#### 5. Quick Bytes
- 5–10 short bullet updates

#### 6. Personal Insight
- Short expert analysis focused on what this means for engineers, DevOps, and cloud architects
- Include actionable insights such as cost optimization, infra impact, or opportunities

Rules:
- Only include items from the last 24 hours
- Prioritize signal over noise
- Avoid repeating points within the briefing
- Cite or link every tool/release and major claim when possible
- If there are fewer than 3 truly important items, say so rather than padding

Also save a markdown copy of the briefing to:
`/Users/eramadan/.hermes/cron/output/ai-news-briefing/YYYY-MM-DD-morning.md`
Use today's date in that filename. If the directory does not exist, create it.

jobs.name: Latvian A2 daily training
jobs.prompt:
You are my Latvian A2 exam-prep tutor. Every day, send ONE mini mock exam inspired by the provided PDF and the official Latvian A2 exam structure.

Goal: make the user practice like the real exam every day for 30 days.

Each daily mock exam must include 4 parts:
1) Listening-style task (text-based, since chat cannot play audio): a short announcement/dialogue and 3 multiple-choice or true/false questions.
2) Reading task: a short text/ad/dialogue with 3 questions.
3) Writing task: 1 short writing prompt (2–5 sentences or a simple message/email/sludinājums).
4) Speaking task: 1 simple speaking prompt with 3 example questions the user should answer aloud or in chat.

Rules:
- Keep everything at A2 level.
- Use themes from the PDF: personal information, family, home, work/study, daily routine, time/weather, food, free time, holidays, shopping, health, transport, travel, services.
- Make it feel like a real exam practice, not a lesson.
- Provide a clear answer key for listening/reading/writing where possible.
- Encourage the user to reply with their answers, and then correct them kindly.
- Keep the mock exam concise enough to do daily.
- Continue sequentially by day number until Day 30.
- Do not promise guaranteed passing; instead help the user become exam-ready.
jobs.last_error: RuntimeError: HTTP 429: Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.

jobs.name: Latvian weekly study schedule
jobs.prompt:
Every Monday at 09:00, send the user this printable weekly Latvian study schedule in Telegram DM. Keep it concise and easy to read in markdown.

## Latvian Weekly Study Schedule

**Goal:** pass Latvian exam through daily practice.

### Monday — Grammar
- *būt* and present tense
- 10 sentence drills
- 5 question forms

### Tuesday — Cases
- nominative, accusative, locative
- write 10 short sentences
- focus on endings like *Rīgā, skolā, māju, draugu*

### Wednesday — Vocabulary
- daily routine words
- work/study words
- make 10 example sentences

### Thursday — Questions + Negatives
- *Kur...? Ko...? Kad...? Kāpēc...?*
- make 10 questions
- make 5 negative sentences

### Friday — Writing
- write a 5–7 sentence paragraph
- include: *jo, tāpēc, parasti, tomēr*

### Saturday — Mixed Review
- review weak spots
- 20-minute mixed quiz
- correct all mistakes

### Sunday — Mock Test
- one mini exam
- one writing task
- review errors and repeat them

**Daily rule:** 25 minutes total
- 5 min vocabulary
- 10 min grammar
- 5 min writing
- 5 min speaking aloud

**Reminder:** focus on full sentences, not isolated words.

jobs.name: DevOps Cloud Platform News Briefing
jobs.prompt:
You are a technical news aggregation agent specializing in DevOps, Cloud, and Platform Engineering.

Fetch and summarize the latest high-quality news and updates from the last 24 hours only.

Scope (STRICT):
- DevOps practices, tooling, and trends
- AWS (services, releases, cost optimization, architecture updates)
- Kubernetes (core + ecosystem)
- CNCF projects (graduations, releases, updates)
- Platform Engineering (IDPs, internal developer platforms, tooling)

Time constraint:
- Include ONLY items published within the last 24 hours from runtime.
- Ignore anything older.
- Report all published times in UTC.

Deduplication:
- Maintain memory of previously returned items by storing a compact local state file at /Users/eramadan/.hermes/cron/output/devops-cloud-platform-news/state.json.
- Dedupe by title + URL hash.
- Do not include duplicate or near-duplicate content.
- If the same story appears across multiple sources, include only the best source.

Source quality:
- Prioritize official blogs (AWS, Kubernetes, CNCF)
- GitHub releases / changelogs
- Reputable tech media (InfoQ, The New Stack, TechCrunch, Reuters, etc.)
- Engineering blogs with technical substance
- Avoid low-quality SEO/clickbait sites

Research method:
- Use official RSS/Atom feeds and official newsroom pages first.
- Use Google News RSS only as a discovery fallback.
- Verify candidate items with direct source pages when possible.
- Filter strictly to the last 24 hours.

Output format:
### 1. Top Headlines (5–10 items)
For each item include:
- Title
- Source
- Published time (UTC)
- Direct URL
- 2–3 sentence technical summary
- Why it matters (DevOps / Cloud impact)

### 2. Key Trends
- Identify 2–4 emerging patterns from today's news
- Keep this short and technical

### 3. Notable Releases / Updates
- New versions, features, or deprecations
- Keep concise and technical

Constraints:
- No duplication across sections
- No marketing fluff unless it contains real technical substance
- No opinion-only articles without actionable insights
- Max 800–1200 words
- Signal-focused, concise, technical tone

Before drafting, load the prior local state file if it exists. After drafting, update the state file with the items returned in this run so future runs can dedupe against prior results.

jobs.name: Weekly system update
jobs.prompt:
Check all installed packages for updates and run the updates. Do the following:

1. First run `brew outdated` to see what needs updating
2. Check npm global packages with `npm outdated -g --depth=0`
3. Check pip outdated packages with `pip list --outdated`
4. Check gem outdated with `gem outdated`
5. Report a summary of all outdated packages with versions (current vs latest)
6. Then run the updates:
   - `npm update -g`
   - `brew upgrade`
   - `pip install --upgrade pip` if pip has outdated packages
   - `gem update --system` if gem has outdated packages
7. Report a final summary of what was updated and any errors

Format the output clearly with sections for:
- Outdated packages found (before update)
- What was updated (after update)
- Any errors or warnings

Keep it concise but informative. Run all checks and updates automatically without asking for confirmation.
